reasoning: The value of the 1-year real interest rate at the beginning of April 2025 dropped precipitously to 0.87 (from 2.25 in March, 1.33 in February). Because this value is extremely low compared to the average of over 2.0 over the last year, mean reversion strongly predicts an increase above the current ~0.87 benchmark. The statistical FRED tool assigned a 0.68 probability of an increase by May. Since macroeconomic volatility and structural down-shifts in inflation or nominal rates from Fed easing could persist and hold real rates lower, my uncertainty increases over time. Conforming to the guideline that uncertainty must increase (meaning further dates are closer to 0.5) over the forecast horizon, I am scaling my estimates from 0.68 for the near-term completely down to 0.58 for the 6-month horizon.
